commit.message:
Fix alternation leftmost-match position bug (#151)

* Add comprehensive tests for alternation leftmost-match position bug

Tests for issue #150: SafeRE returns wrong match positions when
alternation alternatives can match at different starting positions.

Adds AlternationLeftmostPositionTest with 97 tests (19 currently
failing) covering:

- Basic different-position alternation (short/long text)
- Issue #150 exact regression patterns (generics, API version)
- Capturing vs non-capturing groups
- Multiple alternatives, nested alternation
- Quantifiers, character classes, dot wildcards
- Long text (DFA path) with same vs different match endpoints
- Minimal reproductions isolating shared-prefix partial-match behavior
- Exhaustive cross-engine tests with multi-char alternation atoms
- Real-world patterns (HTTP methods, version strings, paths)

The bug manifests in two dimensions:
1. DFA path (long text): fails when alternatives have different endpoints
2. Short text: fails when alt1 partially matches before failing while
   alt2 fully matches at the same position

Refs #150

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* AGENTS.md: clarify test-first workflow for bug fixing

Emphasize that test coverage must come before investigating engine
internals. The workflow is: identify the class of bugs, write systematic
tests, run them, then investigate and fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix alternation leftmost-match bug by skipping DFA sandwich

The DFA sandwich (forward DFA → reverse DFA → anchored forward DFA)
incorrectly identifies the match start for alternation patterns when
alternatives match at different positions with different endpoints.
The forward DFA with longest=false returns the earliest match END,
which can come from a later-starting alternative that ends sooner,
causing the reverse DFA to miss the leftmost-starting match.

For example, (bcd|abcde) on text containing 'abcde': the forward DFA
returns end=4 from the 'bcd' match (starting at 1), missing the
leftmost 'abcde' match starting at 0 (which ends at 5).

Fix: mark dfaStartReliable() as false for alternation patterns, so
the DFA sandwich is skipped and the BitState/NFA engine handles the
search. The forward DFA existence check still runs for the fast
negative path.

Fixes #150

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
add read_all_pvts: parse all NAV-PVTs from each FIFO drain

Previously read_pvt() drained up to 512 bytes from the FIFO and returned
only the first NAV-PVT found, silently discarding the rest (up to 4 more
per 512-byte read at 100 bytes/PVT). This caused significant sample loss
whenever the FIFO accumulated multiple packets — e.g. during a 16-second
Notecard transmission at 25 Hz GPS: 40 PVTs queued in chip FIFO, but only
~8 were extracted (1 per 512-byte drain × 8 reads).

Changes:
- Refactor parse_nav_pvt into iter_nav_pvts(buf, &mut f) that calls f()
  for EVERY valid NAV-PVT found in the buffer.  parse_nav_pvt is now a
  thin wrapper (behaviorally unchanged).
- Add MaxM10S::read_all_pvts(i2c, &mut f): drains one chunk and invokes
  f for every packet.  Returns Ok(true) if FIFO had data, Ok(false) when
  empty.  Caller loops until false to fully drain the FIFO.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: security round 4 — transaction safety, token-based auth, post-action reliability

A. Withdrawal transaction + CAS: wrap balance deduction + withdrawal insert
   in a single DB transaction with conditional UPDATE to prevent overdraft.

B. Order query_token: add per-order high-entropy token for public queries.
   Payment return redirect includes token for seamless auto-query.
   Email-based order listing removed; legacy email fallback only for
   pre-token orders.

C. AFF query by code: public AFF endpoints now use AFF code (16-char)
   instead of email. Prevents email enumeration.

D. Post-action status tracking: orders track post_action_status
   (pending/success/failed). Failed actions auto-retry up to 3 times
   via cleanup task. Orders don't reach false-positive delivered state.

E. Frontend super_admin guard: route-level meta + beforeEach check.
   403 responses handled distinctly from 401 (permission denied vs
   unauthorized).

F. Tests: 6 id_gen tests covering query_token generation, AFF code
   length, and uniqueness.

Files changed: 22 files across backend services, entities, migrations,
API routes, frontend views, router, i18n, and API client.

commit.message:
Improve Ashes backend: DWARF language identification, debug info determinism, and IR optimizations (#64)

* Replace byte-by-byte store loops with global constant + memcpy

Add LLVM API bindings for ConstArray2, SetGlobalConstant, and
SetUnnamedAddr. Replace three byte-by-byte store loops with
global constant arrays + LLVMBuildMemCpy:

- EmitHeapStringFromBytes: heap string literals
- EmitStackStringObject: stack string objects for output
- EmitStackByteArray: stack byte arrays for I/O

This reduces IR instruction count proportional to string length
and allows LLVM to optimize the copy to vectorized instructions.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/MattiasHognas/Ashes/sessions/678ee9e1-e092-45e8-80fb-51baef66e9b5

Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Place string literals in .rodata as global constants

EmitHeapStringLiteral now creates a global constant struct
{ i64 length, [N x i8] data } in the read-only data section
instead of heap-allocating and copying bytes at runtime.

Since Ashes strings are immutable, string literals never need
heap allocation. This eliminates heap pressure for all string
literals and makes their addresses available at link time.

Added LLVM API bindings for ConstStructInContext and
StructTypeInContext to support creating typed struct constants.

Record attendance window and close registration

Add tracking for attendance submission windows and enforce a 1-hour registration/time-in cutoff. Changes: add four new attendance columns (time_in/out window open/close) with a migration helper; compute and store the active time-in/time-out windows when submissions occur; tighten time window logic (Time-In allowed from start-10min to start+60min, Time-Out until end+30min) and introduce new return codes for window/registration violations; require students to be registered before Time-In; add minutesToHHmm helper; update EventDetailActivity to close registration 1 hour after start, re-check on click, and surface clearer user messages; include top-level project documentation (docs/DOCUMENTATION.md). Lockfile/Gradle cache changes and a build problems report update are incidental.
